authoritative_sources.title: Threading Your Way Through the Labeling Requirements Under the Textile and Wool Acts
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. federal authority for Textile and Wool Act labeling rules; this guide defines the required fiber-content, country-of-origin, and manufacturer or RN disclosures for apparel products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Apparel product record and label-baseline review
used_for: blocking unsafe relabel or incomplete-record handling

authoritative_sources.title: Clothes Captioning: Complying with the Care Labeling Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's care-labeling guidance is the official federal workflow reference for permanent care instructions, covered apparel products, and relevant exceptions.
used_for: keeping apparel workflow distinct from generic SKU handling

authoritative_sources.title: Overview of U.S. Requirements for Textiles and Apparel
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. apparel safety regulator under the Flammable Fabrics Act and related consumer-product rules; this official guide ties textile and apparel workflows to applicable testing and certificate controls.
used_for: separating availability support from safety release authority

authoritative_sources.title: Children's Sleepwear Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official CPSC apparel guidance defines category-specific testing and documentation rules for children's sleepwear, a product class with materially different safety gates from ordinary apparel replenishment.

authoritative_sources.title: Informed Compliance Publications
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP is the primary U.S. import enforcement authority and its informed-compliance library is the official trade reference for apparel marking, origin, and classification workflow expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Import, origin, and trade-document prerequisite review

authoritative_sources.title: What Every Member of the Trade Community Should Know About: Marking Requirements for Wearing Apparel
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This apparel-specific CBP publication is an official public source for country-of-origin marking requirements and the import-document expectations that affect relabeling and market-release decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act Operational Guidance for Importers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP's UFLPA importer guidance is the authoritative U.S. baseline for supply-chain tracing, due-diligence evidence, and detention-risk handling that materially affects textile and apparel sourcing decisions.
used_for: why incomplete upstream traceability blocks progression

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 US Apparel and General Merchandise
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US is the recognized standards body for retail identifiers and supply-chain data exchange; this apparel guidance ties GTIN, barcode, RFID, EDI, invoice, and ASN workflows directly to apparel operations.
used_for: ASN, invoice, and inventory workflow terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Exchanging Raw Material Attributes Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This GS1 US apparel guideline is a recognized workflow reference for standardizing raw-material attributes across trading partners, which directly affects apparel tech-pack, sourcing, and substitution evidence.
used_for: fabric or trim substitution evidence expectations
keeping sourcing decisions anchored to named apparel artifacts

workflow_stages.name: Apparel intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the style, colorway, size curve, season, channel, authoritative systems of record, tech-pack revision, and tenant authority map before any availability or replenishment judgment is made.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Availability and replenishment assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare PLM, ERP, vendor, ASN, WMS, OMS, and POS evidence to decide whether the requested apparel action is supportable at the style-color-size level.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Vendor action and release-gate handling
workflow_stages.description: Package bounded procurement, replenishment, and vendor follow-up recommendations while preserving supplier-approval, quality-release, import-control, and spend boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception escalation for shortages, label blocks, and channel exceptions
workflow_stages.description: Stop or escalate when shortages, traceability gaps, relabel requests, franchise exceptions, or children's-product and import-control facts materially change the safe answer.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: PLM product records, tech packs, BOMs, graded specs, fit comments, and revision history
style-color-size matrices, assortment plans, pack ratios, size curves, and channel demand records
fiber-content, care-label, RN, country-of-origin, and packaging approvals
purchase requests, purchase orders, costing summaries, vendor acknowledgements, MOQ commitments, and ex-factory dates
GTIN or UPC item masters, color and size code mappings, carton labels, ASN files, invoices, and warehouse receipts
inventory by style, colorway, and size across WMS, ERP, OMS, POS, and marketplace systems
flammability results, children's-product certificates, quality holds, release records, and compliance test packages
trade-compliance and import-traceability evidence, detention-risk documentation, and vendor due-diligence files
markdown, promotion, franchise exception, return-to-stock, and escalation notes produced by the lane
systems_of_record: PLM or product data management system

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for apparel availability, replenishment, and vendor-readiness support
summarize style, colorway, size, vendor, labeling, origin, and channel blockers with explicit evidence references
prepare bounded procurement, replenishment, and escalation packages without implying final pricing, compliance, customs, or safety authority
escalate: fabric, trim, factory, country-of-origin, or packaging substitutions requiring sourcing, compliance, customs, or legal judgment
children's-product, flammability, release, or detention-risk questions requiring product-compliance, quality, or trade authority
franchise, pricing, markdown, return-policy, or relabel exceptions that exceed documented lane authority
refuse: bypass supplier approval, spend approval, label signoff, release gates, or import-traceability controls to preserve schedule
represent unreconciled style-color-size inventory or ASN data as available for sale or replenishment
treat the apparel industry label as cosmetic and collapse product lifecycle work into generic inventory or merchandising advice

domain_failure_modes: A style is treated as available even though size-curve, colorway, or pack-ratio evidence is incomplete across PLM, vendor, and warehouse systems.
A vendor or schedule slip is answered with a generic substitution suggestion that ignores fabric, trim, labeling, origin, or customs implications.
Inventory is reported as saleable even though ASN, carton, GTIN, or origin-marking evidence does not match the authoritative style-color-size records.
Children's or flammability-sensitive apparel is allowed to progress without the product-specific evidence that makes the category materially different from generic apparel.
Franchise, markdown, returns, or relabel requests are handled as routine workflow changes instead of being routed to the pricing, policy, or compliance owner.
The lane overreaches by approving spend, pricing, supplier changes, quality release, or trade-sensitive decisions that belong to adjacent specialists.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required supply is unavailable or short
Vendor or substitution decision exceeds documented authority
Traceability or inventory evidence is incomplete

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Country-of-origin and trade-preference rules vary by product construction, sourcing path, and import program
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses FTC and CBP public baselines, but runtime must know the actual sourcing countries, manufacturing steps, and entry program before finalizing origin-sensitive guidance.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Children's-product and flammability obligations vary materially by age grading, garment category, and fabric type
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack names CPSC baselines and refuses confident handling when category-specific facts are missing; runtime must retrieve the exact product class before acting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant franchise, markdown, return-to-stock, and supplier-approval policies differ materially across channels and brands
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline constrains the lane, but tenant authority maps still determine whether a given pricing, return, or vendor action is supported or must be escalated.

notes: The source mix covers named-industry apparel labeling and import guidance, recognized apparel data-exchange workflow references, and evidence or control standards for safety and traceability.

No unresolved ambiguity remains blocking for a validated spec pack because the package explicitly requires tenant retrieval of sourcing context, product category, channel policy, and authority maps before final action.
